qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Command injection via git worktree branch name through shell-enabled execFile
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a workspace authorization bypass in @grackle-ai/mcp's knowledge_search and knowledge_get_node tools (packages/mcp/src/tools/knowledge.ts) where authContext is not enforced for scoped agents. The finding describes a command injection in PowerLine's worktree module (packages/powerline/src/worktree.ts) via shell-enabled execFile with unsanitized branch names. These are different packages, different vulnerability classes (authorization bypass vs. command injection), different root causes (missing authContext vs. shell metacharacter injection), and different affected code paths.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a workspace authorization bypass in @grackle-ai/mcp's knowledge_search and knowledge_get_node MCP tools where handlers do not receive authContext and do not enforce workspace scoping (packages/mcp/src/tools/knowledge.ts:146-169, 244-283). The sole qualifying finding describes a command injection in PowerLine's worktree module (packages/powerline/src/worktree.ts) via shell-enabled execFile. These are different packages, different root causes, and different vulnerability classes.
evidence: Advisory vulnerable code: packages/mcp/src/tools/knowledge.ts (knowledge_search handler at lines 146-169, knowledge_get_node handler at lines 244-283)
Advisory root cause: handlers do not receive authContext and do not override workspaceId for scoped callers
Finding vulnerable code: packages/powerline/src/worktree.ts (NODE_GIT_EXECUTOR with shell:true, branch parameter unsanitized)
Finding root cause: shell metacharacter injection via branch name in git worktree commands
No overlap in vulnerable function, package, vulnerability class, or root-cause mechanism
